# SMTA Better-Auth adapter — integration punch-list

**Date:** 2026-06-23
**Audited file:** `SMTA-better-auth-1782169582820.sql` (5,590 lines), generated by the updated `@smta/cli` better-auth adapter.
**Target environment:** Better-Auth on **plain PostgreSQL 18** (no Supabase / GoTrue / PostgREST).
**Status:** does NOT load — aborts on first `GRANT`. Fixes belong in the **SMTA project**, not in HelmetFires.

## How this was verified

Dry-run against the local DB in a single rolled-back transaction (nothing committed):

```bash
docker exec -i helmetfires_db psql -U helmetfires -d helmetfires \
  -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 --single-transaction < SMTA-better-auth-1782169582820.sql
# -> CREATE SCHEMA x4; NOTICE schema "app" already exists; ERROR: role "authenticated" does not exist
```

`core.*` table count after the dry-run: **0** (confirmed rollback).

## Structural root cause

The file is the **Supabase template with Better-Auth patches appended at the very end**
(lines 5514–5590: real `core.get_current_user_id()`, drop the `auth.users` FK on
`core.users_meta`, replacement `user`-table trigger, `DROP EXTENSION pg_graphql`).

Because the corrective block runs **last**, every Supabase-coupled statement above it
executes — and fails — before the fixes are reached. The adapter needs to **patch in place /
emit corrected DDL**, not append corrections after the broken DDL. It currently only
de-supabases `core.*` identity resolution and leaves the rest of the template intact.

---

## A. Hard load-time failures (script aborts)

### A1 — PostgREST roles never created
33 `GRANT … TO {authenticated|anon|service_role}` + 7 `ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES … {authenticated|service_role}`
(lines 27–38, 1244–1255, …). None of these roles are created; the first grant (line 27) aborts.
Better-Auth has no GoTrue role trio — the backend connects as one app role.
**Fix:** emit `CREATE ROLE` for the roles the grants assume, **or** (preferred) parameterize to a
single app role and drop `anon`/`service_role`.

### A2 — `REFERENCES auth.users(id)` in table DDL
`platform.platform_users.supabase_user_id … REFERENCES auth.users(id)` (line 175) and
`user_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id)` (line 1066). `auth.users` doesn't exist under Better-Auth;
`CREATE TABLE` fails. The tail only drops the *`core.users_meta`* FK, not these.
**Fix:** reference the Better-Auth `user` table / `core.users_meta`, or drop the FK as done for `users_meta`.

### A3 — `auth.uid()` in `LANGUAGE sql` function bodies
e.g. `platform.is_platform_user()`, `is_platform_super_admin()` (lines 1738, 1759), line 5022.
SQL-language bodies are validated at `CREATE` time → `auth.uid()` unknown → fail.
**Fix:** replace `auth.uid()` → `core.get_current_user_id()` (18 occurrences total).

### A4 — `CREATE TRIGGER … ON auth.users`
`trg_on_auth_user_created` (lines 872–874) targets a nonexistent table → fails at load.
The tail provides a Better-Auth `user`-table replacement, but the Supabase trigger is still emitted.
**Fix:** don't emit the `auth.users` trigger in Better-Auth mode.

---

## B. Semantic mismatches (may load, but wrong for Better-Auth)

### B5 — `platform.*` schema left fully Supabase-coupled
The adapter de-supabased `core.*` (tail block) but not `platform.*`: `supabase_user_id` column
(lines 175, 192, 243, 1743, 1759, 4511, 5022), `auth.uid()` resolution, and `auth.users` existence
checks in plpgsql bodies (lines 3043, 4555, 4598 — deferred to call-time, so they throw at runtime
not load).
**Fix:** rename `supabase_user_id` → `user_id` (FK to `user`/`core.users_meta`), swap
`auth.uid()` → `core.get_current_user_id()`, swap `auth.users` lookups → `core.users_meta`.

### B6 — `create_platform_user(p_supabase_user_id …)`
Lines 4532–4568: Supabase-named param + `auth.users` existence check (line 4555).
**Fix:** rename param, validate against `core.users_meta`/`user`.

### B7 — Secrets adapter stubs unresolved
`core.store_secret_impl` / `core.delete_secret_impl` still RAISE "not implemented" (lines 69, 77).
The adapter overrode `get_current_user_id` but **not** the secrets provider. Anything calling them
throws at runtime.
**Fix:** ship a Better-Auth secrets impl, or explicitly document secrets as unsupported.

### B8 — Better-Auth user IDs vs UUID
The replacement trigger casts `NEW.id::UUID` (line ~5567) and `core.users_meta.id` is UUID.
Better-Auth's **default** `id` is a random *string* (cuid-style), not a UUID — the cast throws on
every signup.
**Fix:** the adapter must require/document Better-Auth `advanced.database.generateId` producing
UUIDs (or store the id as text). Make this an explicit prerequisite.

### B9 — `anon` / `service_role` grants are dead concepts
Better-Auth has no anonymous DB role and no bypass-RLS service role; any `TO anon` /
`TO service_role` grant (and the `platform` default-privs at 1244–1255) maps to nothing.
**Fix:** decide the real connection identities; drop or remap.

---

## C. HelmetFires-integration concern (not an SMTA bug, but flag it)

### C10 — `ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA app GRANT SELECT … TO authenticated` (line 36)
Even once roles exist, this auto-grants SELECT on *every future `app` table* to the broad role.
HelmetFires exposes `app` **only** through the `api` schema (app role gets `USAGE` on `api`, never
direct table access on `app`). This silently widens access on all HF tables.
**Decision:** whether SMTA should own default-privs on the shared `app` schema at all, or leave that
to the consuming project.

### C11 — `DROP EXTENSION IF EXISTS pg_graphql` (line 5512)
Harmless here (guarded), but a Supabase-ism worth pruning in non-Supabase output.

---

## Not problems (don't chase these)

- The real `core.get_current_user_id()` reading `current_setting('app.current_user_id', true)` is
  **correct** (lines 5522–5524) — exactly the HelmetFires tenant-context design.
- **No RLS policy is role-targeted** (0 `CREATE POLICY … TO <role>`) — policies apply to PUBLIC, so
  the role issue is confined to `GRANT` / `ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES`, not policy applicability.

## Summary of what the SMTA Better-Auth adapter must do

1. Don't emit the GoTrue role trio (or emit `CREATE ROLE` for them).
2. De-supabase `platform.*` the same way `core.*` was handled.
3. Generate corrected DDL **in place** rather than appending fixes after broken DDL.
4. Supply the secrets impl + document the UUID-id requirement.
